title: Attention-Like Hebbian Learning from Quantum Probability Flow and Quantum-Annealer Tests
abstract: We propose a quantum probability-flow principle for deriving local learning rules in associative memory. A transverse field defines leakage channels from data states, and minimizing the measured survival loss gives stability-driven updates. For imaginary-time, dephased dynamics, the local leakage free energy is the log-sum-exp of energy gaps; its gradient is a softmax-weighted Hebbian rule. Real-time stability instead yields a power-law weighting. D-Wave standard- and fast-anneal tests of a one-hot attention forward map are better fitted by an effective softmax than by a Lorentzian power law.
summary: This work introduces a quantum probability-flow principle to derive learning rules for associative memory, showing that the attention mechanism emerges naturally from minimizing the quantum leakage loss. The resulting update rule is a softmax-weighted Hebbian rule, which is validated by comparing theoretical predictions with actual measurements on a quantum annealer.
main_problem: The paper seeks a physical principle to derive local learning rules that naturally interpolate between classical Hebbian learning and modern attention mechanisms for associative memory.
main_result: The attention-like weighting emerges naturally from minimizing a quantum probability-flow loss, which is governed by the local energy gaps of the system.
method: The authors propose a quantum probability-flow principle, analyzing the gradient of the survival loss derived from imaginary-time quantum dynamics.
model_or_system: The model is an Ising memory described by a Hamiltonian, analyzed using quantum probability flow principles applied to data patterns as basis states.
key_observables: Local energy gaps ($\Delta E_k$), the quantum probability-flow loss ($\ell_ eta(x)$), and the resulting softmax-weighted Hebbian update rule.
important_parameters: The inverse temperature parameter ($ eta$), which controls the transition between standard Hebbian learning ($ eta 	o 0$) and the hard-attention limit ($ eta 	o \infty$).
main_assumptions: The analysis relies on the imaginary-time, dephased, single-flip approximation for deriving the core learning rule.
figures_summary: Table I compares D-Wave annealer test results for a spin formulation, fitting global KL divergences to both softmax and power-law models across different anneal protocols.
paper_structure: The paper develops the quantum probability-flow principle, derives the softmax-weighted Hebbian update rule, analyzes the temperature limits, and concludes with hardware tests on a quantum annealer to validate the softmax fit.
why_interesting: It connects fundamental concepts of quantum stability and energy gaps to the highly successful, but often ad-hoc, mathematical structures of modern deep learning attention mechanisms.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
